title.zh: 卷二十二 朱景王杜馬劉傅堅馬列傳
title.en: Volume 22: Biographies of Zhu, Jing, Wang, Du, Ma, Liu, Fu, Jian, Ma
title.raw: 卷二十二 朱景王杜馬劉傅堅馬列傳 Volume 22: Biographies of Zhu, Jing, Wang, Du, Ma, Liu, Fu, Jian, Ma

sentences.zh: 朱祐字仲先，南陽宛人也。

translations.literal: Zhu You, courtesy name Zhongxian, was a native of Wan in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You, styled Zhongxian, came from Wan in Nanyang commandery.

sentences.zh: 少孤，歸外家復陽劉氏，往來舂陵，世祖與伯升皆親愛之。

translations.literal: In youth he was orphaned and went to his maternal kin, the Liu clan of Fuyang; he traveled back and forth to Chunling, and the Shizu and Bosheng both loved him dearly.
translations.idiomatic: Orphaned young, he was raised among his mother's people, the Lius of Fuyang, and often visited Chunling, where Liu Xiu and his elder brother Liu Yan treated him as family.

sentences.zh: 伯升拜大司徒，以祐為護軍。

translations.literal: When Bosheng was appointed grand minister of education, he made You protector of the army.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Yan became grand minister of education, he named Zhu You protector of the army.

sentences.zh: 及世祖為大司馬，討河北，復以祐為護軍，常見親幸，舍止於中。

translations.literal: When the Shizu became grand marshal and campaigned in Hebei, he again made You protector of the army; You was constantly shown intimate favor and lodged within headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Xiu became grand marshal for the Hebei campaign, he again made Zhu You his protector of the army; Zhu You enjoyed unusual access and even slept inside headquarters.

sentences.zh: 祐侍宴，從容曰：『長安政亂，公有日角之相，此天命也。

translations.literal: At a banquet You said at ease: "Chang'an is in political chaos; you have the physiognomy of the sun-horn—this is Heaven's mandate."
translations.idiomatic: Over wine Zhu You said quietly, "Chang'an is falling apart, and you bear the sun-horn mark of sovereignty—that is Heaven's choice."

sentences.zh: 』世祖曰：『召刺奸收護軍！

translations.literal: The Shizu said: "Summon the investigator of malfeasance to arrest the protector of the army!"
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiu shot back, "Tell the investigator of crimes to clap the protector of the army in irons!"

sentences.zh: 』祐乃不敢復言。

translations.literal: You thereupon dared not speak again.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You never raised the subject again.

sentences.zh: 從征河北，常力戰陷陣，以為偏將軍，封安陽侯。

translations.literal: He followed the Hebei campaign, constantly fighting hard and breaking enemy lines; he was appointed lieutenant general and enfeoffed as marquis of Anyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the Hebei wars he fought at the sharp end and was named lieutenant general and marquis of Anyang.

sentences.zh: 世祖即位，拜為建義大將軍。

translations.literal: When the Shizu ascended the throne, he appointed him grand general who establishes righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: At Guangwu's accession Zhu You became grand general who establishes righteousness.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，更封堵陽侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu he was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Duoyang.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 his fief was moved to Duoyang.

sentences.zh: 冬，與諸將擊鄧奉於淯陽，祐軍敗，為奉所獲。

translations.literal: In winter, together with the generals he struck Deng Feng at Yuyang; You's army was defeated and You was captured by Feng.
translations.idiomatic: That winter he joined the attack on Deng Feng at Yuyang; Zhu You's command was routed and Deng Feng took him prisoner.

sentences.zh: 明年，奉破，乃肉袒因祐降。

translations.literal: The next year, when Feng was broken, Feng bared his upper body and through You submitted.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, after Deng Feng's defeat, Deng Feng stripped to the waist and surrendered through Zhu You's mediation.

sentences.zh: 帝復祐位而厚加慰賜。

translations.literal: The Emperor restored You's rank and richly consoled and rewarded him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor restored his post and heaped gifts and reassurance on him.

sentences.zh: 遣擊新野、隨，皆平之。

translations.literal: He was sent to strike Xinye and Sui; both were pacified.
translations.idiomatic: He was then dispatched against Xinye and Sui; both places were brought to heel.

sentences.zh: 延岑自敗於穰，遂與秦豐將張成合，祐率征虜將軍祭遵與戰於東陽，大破之，臨陣斬成，延岑敗走歸豐。

translations.literal: Yan Cen, after his defeat at Rang, thereupon joined Qin Feng's general Zhang Cheng; You led General Who Subdues Captives Ji Zun and fought them at Dongyang, greatly defeated them, beheaded Cheng on the field, and Yan Cen fled in defeat back to Feng.
translations.idiomatic: After Yan Cen's rout at Rang he merged with Qin Feng's officer Zhang Cheng. Zhu You and General Who Subdues Captives Ji Zun met them at Dongyang, shattered their army, killed Zhang Cheng in the fighting, and drove Yan Cen back to Qin Feng.

sentences.zh: 祐收得印綬九十七。

translations.literal: You collected ninety-seven seals and cords.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You gathered ninety-seven official seals from the field.

sentences.zh: 進擊黃郵，降之，賜祐黃金三十斤。

translations.literal: He advanced to strike Huangyou, submitted him, and was gifted thirty jin of gold.
translations.idiomatic: He next reduced Huangyou, who capitulated, and the throne rewarded him with thirty jin of gold.

sentences.zh: 四年，率破奸將軍侯進、輔威將軍耿植代征南大將軍岑彭圍秦豐於黎丘，破其將張康於蔡陽，斬之。

translations.literal: In the fourth year he led General Who Breaks Treachery Hou Jin and General Who Aids Awe Geng Zhi to replace Grand General Who Conquers the South Cen Peng in besieging Qin Feng at Liqiu; he defeated Feng's general Zhang Kang at Caiyang and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 4 he took over Cen Peng's siege of Qin Feng at Liqiu with Hou Jin and Geng Zhi, crushed Zhang Kang at Caiyang, and struck off his head.

sentences.zh: 帝自至黎丘，使御史中丞李由持璽書招豐，豐出惡言，不肯降。

translations.literal: The Emperor himself came to Liqiu and sent Imperial Secretary Li You with an imperial writ to summon Feng; Feng uttered vile language and refused to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu came in person to Liqiu and sent Imperial Secretary Li You with a sealed summons; Qin Feng answered with abuse and would not yield.

sentences.zh: 車駕引還，敕祐方略，祐盡力攻之。

translations.literal: The imperial carriage led the army back and ordered You on strategy; You exhausted his strength in attacking.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor withdrew his train, he left Zhu You detailed orders; Zhu You threw everything into the assault.

sentences.zh: 明年夏，城中窮困，豐乃將其母、妻、子九人肉袒降。

translations.literal: The next summer the city was reduced to extremity; Feng then led his mother, wife, and nine children, baring their upper bodies, to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: By the following summer the city was starving. Qin Feng led his mother, wife, and nine children out bare-chested to surrender.

sentences.zh: 祐監車傳豐送洛陽，斬之。

translations.literal: You supervised the cart that conveyed Feng to Luoyang and had him executed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You escorted him to Luoyang in a prison wagon and had him executed.

sentences.zh: 大司馬吳漢劾奏祐廢詔受降，違將帥之任，帝不加罪。

translations.literal: Grand Marshal Wu Han impeached You for disregarding the edict in accepting surrender and violating a commander's duty; the Emperor did not punish him.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Marshal Wu Han memorialized that Zhu You had disobeyed orders by accepting the surrender; the emperor took no action against him.

sentences.zh: 祐還，與騎都尉臧宮會擊延岑餘黨陰、酂、築陽三縣賊，悉平之。

translations.literal: You returned and with Colonel of Cavalry Zang Gong joined in striking Yan Cen's remnant faction, the bandits of the three counties of Yin, Zan, and Zhuyang, and all were pacified.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You then teamed with Zang Gong to mop up Yan Cen's diehards in Yin, Zan, and Zhuyang until every pocket was cleared.

sentences.zh: 祐為人質直，尚儒學。

translations.literal: You as a man was honest and blunt and esteemed Confucian learning.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You was plain-spoken, upright, and a devoted student of the classics.

sentences.zh: 將兵率眾，多受降，以克定城邑為本，不存首級之功。

translations.literal: In leading troops and commanding masses he often accepted surrenders and took securing cities and towns as his foundation, not storing up merit in counted heads.
translations.idiomatic: As a commander he preferred enemies who yielded and measured success by pacified towns rather than by piles of severed heads.

sentences.zh: 又禁制士卒不得虜掠百姓，軍人樂放縱，多以此怨之。

translations.literal: He also strictly forbade soldiers from plundering the common people; soldiers loved to run wild, and for this many resented him.
translations.idiomatic: He strictly forbade looting, which earned him the resentment of troops who lived for plunder.

sentences.zh: 九年，屯南行唐拒匈奴。

translations.literal: In the ninth year he encamped at Nanxingtang to hold off the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 9 he garrisoned Nanxingtang against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 十三年，增邑，定封鬲侯，食邑七千三百戶。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year his fief was increased and fixed as marquis of Ge, with revenue from seven thousand three hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 13 his income was raised and his title fixed as marquis of Ge, with 7,300 households.

sentences.zh: 十五年，朝京師，上大將軍印綬，因留奉朝請。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year he had audience in the capital, submitted the great general's seal and ribbon, and thereupon remained as fengchaoqing.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 15 he came to court, surrendered the great general's seal, and stayed on as a supernumerary gentleman.

sentences.zh: 祐奏古者人臣受封，不加王爵，可改諸王為公。

translations.literal: You memorialized that in antiquity when ministers received fief they were not given the rank of king; it was possible to change the various kings to dukes.
translations.idiomatic: He memorialized that in ancient times enfeoffed ministers had not borne the title of king, and urged that the imperial princes be redesignated dukes.

sentences.zh: 帝即施行。

translations.literal: The Emperor at once put this into practice.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor adopted the reform immediately.

sentences.zh: 又奏宜令三公並去『大』名，以法經典。

translations.literal: He also memorialized that it was fitting to order the three dukes together to remove the word "grand" from their titles, to accord with the classics.
translations.idiomatic: He further asked that the three highest ministers drop the prefix "grand" from their titles to match classical usage.

sentences.zh: 後遂從其議。

translations.literal: Later the court followed this proposal.
translations.idiomatic: The court later accepted that recommendation as well.

sentences.zh: 祐初學長安，帝往候之，祐不時相勞苦，而先升講舍。

translations.literal: When You first studied in Chang'an, the Emperor went to visit him; You did not first come down to offer greetings but first ascended the lecture hall.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu called on him during his student days in Chang'an, Zhu You went straight to the lecture hall instead of coming out to greet his guest.

sentences.zh: 後車駕幸其第，帝因笑曰：『主人得無舍我講乎？

translations.literal: Later when the imperial carriage visited his residence, the Emperor therefore laughed and said: "Surely the host has not abandoned me for his lecture?"
translations.idiomatic: When the imperial carriage later stopped at his house, the emperor teased, "I hope I am not interrupting your lesson?"

sentences.zh: 』以有舊恩，數蒙賞賚。

translations.literal: Because there was old favor, he repeatedly received gifts and grants.
translations.idiomatic: Their long friendship brought Zhu You frequent imperial largesse.

sentences.zh: 二十四年，卒。

translations.literal: In the twenty-fourth year he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Jianwu 24.

sentences.zh: 子商嗣。

translations.literal: His son Shang succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Zhu Shang inherited the marquisate.

sentences.zh: 商卒，子演嗣，永元十四年，坐以兄伯為外孫陰皇后巫蠱事，免為庶人。

translations.literal: When Shang died, his son Yan succeeded; in the fourteenth year of Yongyuan, because his elder brother Bo was involved in the witchcraft case of Empress Yin as maternal grandson, he was stripped to commoner status.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Shang was succeeded by Zhu Yan, who lost his title in Yongyuan 14 when his brother Zhu Bo was implicated in Empress Yin's witchcraft affair as her grandson by a daughter.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后紹封演子沖為鬲侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Dowager Deng continued the line and enfeoffed Yan's son Chong as marquis of Ge.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Dowager Deng restored the line, naming Zhu Yan's son Zhu Chong marquis of Ge.

sentences.zh: 景丹字孫卿，馮翊櫟陽人也。

translations.literal: Jing Dan, courtesy name Sunqing, was a native of Liyang in Pingyi.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Dan, styled Sunqing, came from Liyang in Pingyi commandery.

sentences.zh: 少學長安。

translations.literal: In youth he studied in Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: He had studied in Chang'an as a young man.

sentences.zh: 王莽時舉四科，丹以言語為固德侯相，有幹事稱，遷朔調連率副貳。

translations.literal: In Wang Mang's time the four subjects were raised; Dan, on the strength of his eloquence, became chancellor to the marquis of Gu de and was praised for administrative ability; he was promoted to deputy of the Shuodiao lianshuai.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang's "four categories" recruitment he won appointment as chancellor to the marquis of Gude on eloquence, earned a name for competence, and rose to deputy intendant of the Shuodiao circuit.

sentences.zh: 更始立，遣使者徇上谷，丹與連率耿況降，復為上谷長史。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, he sent envoys to tour Shanggu; Dan together with Lianshuai Geng Kuang surrendered and again became chief clerk of Shanggu.
translations.idiomatic: When the Gengshi Emperor took power, his envoys reached Shanggu; Jing Dan went over with Intendant Geng Kuang and resumed his old post as chief clerk of the commandery.

sentences.zh: 王郎起，丹與況共謀拒之。

translations.literal: When Wang Lang rose, Dan together with Kuang plotted to resist him.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang rebelled, Jing Dan and Geng Kuang plotted joint resistance.

sentences.zh: 況使丹與子弇及寇恂等將兵南歸世祖，世祖引見丹等，笑曰：『邯鄲將帥數言我發漁陽、上谷兵，吾卿應言然，何意二郡良為吾來！

translations.literal: Kuang sent Dan with his son Yan and Kou Xun and others to lead troops south to join the Shizu; the Shizu led them in audience and laughed, saying: "The Handan generals kept saying I had raised the troops of Yuyang and Shanggu; you gentlemen only answered yes—who would have thought the two commanderies would truly come for me!"
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kuang sent Jing Dan with his son Geng Yan and Kou Xun south to Guangwu, who received them with a laugh: "Wang Lang's people kept insisting I had mobilized Yuyang and Shanggu for myself; you only humored them—yet here you are, both commanderies in person!"

sentences.zh: 方與士大夫共此功名耳。

translations.literal: I am about to share this achievement and fame with gentlemen and grandees."
translations.idiomatic: Now we may win this renown together."

sentences.zh: 』拜丹為偏將軍，號奉義侯。

translations.literal: He appointed Dan lieutenant general with the title marquis who honors righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: He named Jing Dan lieutenant general and marquis who honors righteousness.

sentences.zh: 從擊王郎將宏等於南□，郎兵迎戰，漢軍退卻，丹等縱突騎擊，大破之，追奔十餘里，死傷者從橫。

translations.literal: He followed in striking Wang Lang's general Hong and others at Nanluan (the transmitted text has a lacuna here); Lang's troops met them in battle, the Han army fell back, but Dan and the others unleashed shock cavalry, greatly defeated the enemy, pursued them for over ten li, and dead and wounded lay strewn about.
translations.idiomatic: At Nanluan—where the place name is damaged in the received text—he helped crush Wang Lang's officer Hong. The enemy pressed hard until Jing Dan's shock cavalry countercharged, routed them for more than ten li, and left the field strewn with corpses.

sentences.zh: 丹還，世祖謂曰：『吾聞突騎天下精兵，今乃見其戰，樂可言邪？

translations.literal: When Dan returned, the Shizu said to him: "I have heard that shock cavalry are the realm's elite troops; now I have seen them fight—could joy be put in words?"
translations.idiomatic: When Jing Dan rode back, Guangwu told him, "They say shock cavalry are the finest in the empire—today I saw why; no words can describe the exhilaration."

sentences.zh: 』遂從征河北。

translations.literal: Thereupon he followed in the Hebei campaign.
translations.idiomatic: From then on he campaigned through the pacification of Hebei.

sentences.zh: 世祖即位，以讖文用平狄將軍孫咸行大司馬，眾威不悅。

translations.literal: When the Shizu ascended the throne, because of a prophecy text he used General Who Pacifies the Di Sun Xian to act as grand marshal; the host of officials were all displeased.
translations.idiomatic: At the accession a cryptic text had pointed to Sun Xian, general who pacifies the Di, as acting grand marshal—a choice that dismayed the court.

sentences.zh: 詔舉可為大司馬者，群臣所推惟吳漢及丹。

translations.literal: An edict ordered the raising of candidates for grand marshal; what the ministers pushed was only Wu Han and Dan.
translations.idiomatic: An edict called for nominations; the ministers could agree only on Wu Han and Jing Dan.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：『景將軍北州大將，是其人也。

translations.literal: The Emperor said: "General Jing is the great general of the northern provinces—that is the man."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "General Jing commands the northern heartland—he fits the bill."

sentences.zh: 然吳將軍有建大策之勛，又誅苗幽州、謝尚書，其功大。

translations.literal: Yet General Wu has the merit of framing the great strategy, and also executed Miao of Youzhou and Minister Xie; his merit is the greater.
translations.idiomatic: Yet General Wu Han devised the grand strategy and killed Miao of Youzhou and Minister Xie—his is the greater service."

sentences.zh: 舊制驃騎將軍官與大司馬相兼也。

translations.literal: By old regulation the office of general of agile cavalry and that of grand marshal were mutually combined.
translations.idiomatic: By precedent the posts of general of agile cavalry and grand marshal had been paired."

sentences.zh: 』乃以吳漢為大司馬，而拜丹為驃騎大將軍。

translations.literal: Thereupon he made Wu Han grand marshal and appointed Dan grand general of agile cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: So Wu Han became grand marshal and Jing Dan grand general of agile cavalry.

sentences.zh: 子尚嗣，徙封余吾侯。

translations.literal: His son Shang succeeded and was moved in fief to marquis of Yuwu.
translations.idiomatic: His son Jing Shang inherited and was transferred to the marquisate of Yuwu.

sentences.zh: 尚卒，子苞嗣。

translations.literal: When Shang died, his son Bao succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Jing Shang died, his son Jing Bao succeeded.

sentences.zh: 苞卒，子臨嗣，無子，國絕。

translations.literal: When Bao died, his son Lin succeeded; there was no son, and the state was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Bao was followed by Jing Lin, who left no heir, and the line ended.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后紹封苞弟遽為監亭侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Dowager Deng continued the line and enfeoffed Bao's younger brother Qu as marquis of Jianting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Dowager Deng renewed the fief for Jing Bao's brother Jing Qu as marquis of Jianting.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，與大司馬吳漢等俱擊檀鄉，有詔軍事一屬大司馬，而梁輒發野王兵，帝以其不奉詔敕，令止在所縣，而梁復以便宜進軍。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu, together with Grand Marshal Wu Han and others he struck Tanxiang; there was an edict that military affairs should all be subordinate to the grand marshal, yet Liang sent troops from Yewang; the Emperor because he did not obey the edict ordered him to halt in the county where he was, yet Liang again on his own authority advanced the army.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 Wang Liang joined Wu Han against the Tanxiang rebels under orders that all operations answer to the grand marshal; Wang Liang nonetheless called out the Yewang garrison. Guangwu told him to hold his position, but Wang Liang again advanced on his own authority.

sentences.zh: 帝以梁前後違命，大怒，遣尚書宗廣持節軍中斬梁。

translations.literal: The Emperor because Liang front and back violated orders was greatly angry and sent Minister of the Shangshu Zong Guang with the staff to the army to execute Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Furious at repeated disobedience, Guangwu sent Minister Zong Guang with the vermilion staff to behead Wang Liang in camp.

sentences.zh: 廣不忍，乃檻車送京師。

translations.literal: Guang could not bear it and therefore sent him in a caged cart to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Zong Guang could not bring himself to strike and sent Wang Liang to the capital in a prison cart instead.

sentences.zh: 既至，赦之。

translations.literal: When he arrived, he was pardoned.
translations.idiomatic: Once there, he received a pardon.

sentences.zh: 月餘，以為中郎將，行執金吾事。

translations.literal: After more than a month he was appointed middle general and acted in the duties of bearer of the gilded mace.
translations.idiomatic: A month later he was named middle general and acting bearer of the gilded mace.

sentences.zh: 北守箕關，擊赤眉別校，降之。

translations.literal: He guarded Ji Pass in the north, struck a detached command of the Red Eyebrows, and submitted them.
translations.idiomatic: He held Ji Pass against the north, smashed a Red Eyebrows detachment, and took their surrender.

sentences.zh: 三年春，轉擊五校，追至信都、趙國，破之，悉平諸屯聚。

translations.literal: In the spring of the third year he turned to strike the Five Camps, pursued them to Xindu and Zhao, broke them, and entirely pacified the various fortified camps.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 3 he turned on the Five Camps, chased them through Xindu and Zhao, and wiped out their strongholds.

sentences.zh: 冬，遣使者持節拜梁前將軍。

translations.literal: In winter an envoy with the staff appointed Liang forward general.
translations.idiomatic: That winter an imperial messenger with the staff commissioned him forward general.

sentences.zh: 四年春，擊肥城、文陽，拔之。

translations.literal: In the spring of the fourth year he struck Feicheng and Wenyang and took them.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 4 he took Feicheng and Wenyang.

sentences.zh: 進與驃騎大將軍杜茂擊佼韁、蘇茂於楚、沛間，拔大梁、嚙桑，而捕虜將軍馬武、偏將軍王霸亦分道並進，歲餘悉平之。

translations.literal: He advanced together with Grand General of Agile Cavalry Du Mao to strike Jiao Qiang and Su Mao between Chu and Pei, took Daliang and Niesang, while General Who Captures Captives Ma Wu and Lieutenant General Wang Ba also advanced by separate routes in concert; within a year all were pacified.
translations.idiomatic: He joined Grand General of Agile Cavalry Du Mao against Jiao Qiang and Su Mao between Chu and Pei, seized Daliang and Niesang, while Ma Wu and Wang Ba converged by other routes; within a year the region was quiet.

sentences.zh: 五年，從救桃城，破龐萌等，梁戰尤力，拜山陽太守，鎮撫新附，將兵如故。

translations.literal: In the fifth year he followed in rescuing Taocheng, broke Pang Meng and others; Liang fought especially hard, was appointed governor of Shanyang, pacified the new adherents, and led troops as before.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 5 he helped relieve Taocheng and crush Pang Meng, fighting with conspicuous ferocity; he was named governor of Shanyang to settle the new adherents while keeping his command.

sentences.zh: 數月征人，代歐陽歙為河南尹。

translations.literal: After several months he was summoned and replaced Ouyang She as governor of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Within months he was recalled to replace Ouyang She as governor of Henan.

sentences.zh: 梁穿渠引穀水註洛陽城下，東寫鞏川，及渠成而水不流。

translations.literal: Liang dug a canal to lead the Gu River in under the walls of Luoyang and drain it east into the Gong river; when the canal was finished the water would not flow.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Liang cut a channel to divert the Gu into Luoyang and discharge it east toward the Gong River, but when the ditch was done the water refused to run.

sentences.zh: 七年，有司劾奏之，梁慚懼，上書乞骸骨。

translations.literal: In the seventh year the authorities impeached him; Liang was ashamed and afraid and memorialized begging to surrender his bones.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 7 censors impeached him; mortified, Wang Liang asked to retire.

sentences.zh: 乃下詔曰：『梁前將兵征伐，眾人稱賢，故擢典京師。

translations.literal: Thereupon an edict was issued saying: "Liang formerly led troops in campaigns and the masses called him worthy; therefore he was promoted to govern the capital."
translations.idiomatic: The throne replied: "You earned Henan by merit in the field."

sentences.zh: 建議開渠，為人興利，旅力既愆，迄無成功，百姓怨讟，談者讙嘩。

translations.literal: "He proposed opening a canal to create benefit for the people; joint strength already failed, and finally there was no success; the hundred surnames resent and slander, and talkers clamor."
translations.idiomatic: "Your canal was meant to help the people, yet the effort misfired and the people only mutter."

sentences.zh: 雖蒙寬宥，猶執謙退，「君子成人之美」，其以梁為濟南太守。

translations.literal: "Although he received lenient pardon, he still holds humility and withdrawal; a gentleman completes others' beauty—appoint Liang governor of Jinan."
translations.idiomatic: "Still you beg to step down like a true gentleman who would not spoil another's good name. Very well—be governor of Jinan instead."

sentences.zh: 』十三年，增邑，定封阜成侯。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year his fief was increased and fixed as marquis of Fucheng.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 13 his stipend was raised and his title fixed as marquis of Fucheng.

sentences.zh: 十四年，卒官。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in Jianwu 14.

sentences.zh: 子禹嗣。

translations.literal: His son Yu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Wang Yu inherited.

sentences.zh: 禹卒，子堅石嗣。

translations.literal: When Yu died, his son Jianshi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Yu died, his son Wang Jianshi succeeded.

sentences.zh: 堅石追坐父禹及弟平與楚王英謀反，棄市，國除。

translations.literal: Jianshi was implicated because his father Yu and younger brother Ping plotted rebellion with the Prince of Chu Ying; he was executed in the marketplace and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Jianshi was dragged down when his father Wang Yu and brother Wang Ping were found plotting with Prince Ying of Chu; he died on the public scaffold and the marquisate was abolished.

sentences.zh: 杜茂字諸公，南陽冠軍人也。

translations.literal: Du Mao, courtesy name Zhugong, was a native of Guanjun in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Du Mao, styled Zhugong, came from Guanjun in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 初歸光武於河北，為中堅將軍，常從征伐。

translations.literal: At first he joined Guangwu in Hebei as middle-core general and constantly followed in campaigns.
translations.idiomatic: He had joined Liu Xiu in Hebei as middle-core general and fought in every major action.

sentences.zh: 世祖即位，拜大將軍，封樂鄉侯。

translations.literal: When the Shizu ascended the throne, he was appointed grand general and enfeoffed as marquis of Lexiang.
translations.idiomatic: At the accession he became grand general and marquis of Lexiang.

sentences.zh: 北擊五校於真定，進降廣平。

translations.literal: He struck the Five Camps in the north at Zhending and advanced to submit Guangping.
translations.idiomatic: He drove the Five Camps from Zhending northward and brought Guangping to submission.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，更封苦陘侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu he was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Kuxing.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 his fief became Kuxing.

sentences.zh: 與中郎將王梁擊五校賊於魏郡、清河、東郡，悉平諸營保，降其持節大將三十餘人，三郡清靜，道路流通。

translations.literal: Together with Middle General Wang Liang he struck the Five Camps bandits in Wei, Qinghe, and Dong commanderies, entirely pacified all camps and forts, submitted more than thirty great generals bearing credentials, and the three commanderies were quiet and clear with roads open.
translations.idiomatic: With Middle General Wang Liang he cleared the Five Camps from Wei, Qinghe, and Dong, forced thirty-odd credential-bearing enemy generals to yield, and opened the roads across three commanderies.

sentences.zh: 明年，遣使持節拜茂為驃騎大將軍，擊沛郡，拔芒。

translations.literal: The next year an envoy with the staff appointed Mao grand general of agile cavalry; he struck Pei commandery and took Mang.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he received the staff as grand general of agile cavalry, struck Pei, and took Mang.

sentences.zh: 時，西防復反，迎佼彊。

translations.literal: At that time Xifang rebelled again and welcomed Jiao Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: Then Xifang rose again for Jiao Qiang.

sentences.zh: 五年春，茂率捕虜將軍馬武進攻西防，數月拔之，彊奔董憲。

translations.literal: In the spring of the fifth year Mao led General Who Captures Captives Ma Wu in an advance attack on Xifang; after several months he took it, and Qiang fled to Dong Xian.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 5 he and Ma Wu stormed Xifang, took it after a long siege, and drove Jiao Qiang to Dong Xian.

sentences.zh: 東方既平，七年，詔茂引兵北屯田晉陽、廣武，以備胡寇。

translations.literal: When the east had been pacified, in the seventh year an edict ordered Mao to lead troops north to garrison-field at Jinyang and Guangwu, in order to guard against Hu raiders.
translations.idiomatic: After the east was settled, Jianwu 7 ordered him north to open military colonies at Jinyang and Guangwu against the steppe raiders.

sentences.zh: 九年，與雁門太守郭涼擊盧芳將尹由於繁畤，芳將賈覽率胡騎萬餘救之，茂戰，軍敗，引入樓煩城。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, together with Yanmen Governor Guo Liang he struck Lu Fang's general Yin You at Fanzhi; Fang's general Jia Lan led over ten thousand Hu cavalry to rescue them; Mao fought, the army was defeated, and he withdrew into Loufan city.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 9 he and Yanmen governor Guo Liang attacked Lu Fang's general Yin You at Fanzhi until Lu Fang's Jia Lan arrived with ten thousand Hu horse; Du Mao's line broke and he fell back into Loufan.

sentences.zh: 時，盧芳據高柳，與匈奴連兵，數寇邊民，帝患之。

translations.literal: At that time Lu Fang held Gaoliu and joined arms with the Xiongnu, repeatedly raiding the border people; the Emperor worried over this.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang held Gaoliu in league with the Xiongnu and harried the frontier until the court grew alarmed.

sentences.zh: 十二年，遣謁者段忠將眾郡弛刑配茂，鎮守北邊，因發邊卒築亭候，修烽火，又發委輸金帛繒絮供給軍士，並賜邊民，冠蓋相望。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year he sent Yezhe Duan Zhong to lead various commanderies' commuting sentences and assign them to Mao to guard the north border; for that reason he sent border soldiers to build pavilions and watchtowers and repair beacon fires, and also sent transport of gold, silk, and fabrics to supply the soldiers, together gifting the border people, so that official caps and canopies stretched one after another.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 12 Yezhe Duan Zhong led amnestied convicts from several commanderies to reinforce Du Mao's northern line, rebuild signal towers and beacons, and forward gold, silk, and cloth to troops and civilians until relief convoys clogged the roads.

sentences.zh: 茂亦建屯田，驢東轉運。

translations.literal: Mao also established garrison fields and used donkeys to transport eastward.
translations.idiomatic: Du Mao expanded the military farms and ran supply trains of pack donkeys eastward.

sentences.zh: 先是，雁門人賈丹、霍匡、解勝等為尹由所略，由以為將帥，與共守平城。

translations.literal: Previously the Yanmen men Jia Dan, Huo Kuang, and Xie Sheng had been plundered by Yin You; You made them generals and together with them guarded Pingcheng.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier Yanmen natives Jia Dan, Huo Kuang, and Xie Sheng had been pressed into service by Yin You, who made them officers in the defense of Pingcheng.

sentences.zh: 丹等聞芳敗，遂共殺由詣郭涼；

translations.literal: When Dan and the others heard Fang was defeated, thereupon together they killed You and went to Guo Liang;
translations.idiomatic: When Jia Dan and his comrades heard Lu Fang was beaten, they killed Yin You and defected to Guo Liang;

sentences.zh: 涼上狀，皆封為列侯，詔送委輸金帛賜茂、涼軍吏及平城降民。

translations.literal: Liang reported the facts upward; all were enfeoffed as full marquises; an edict sent transport of gold and silk to reward Mao, Liang's army officers, and the surrendered people of Pingcheng.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Liang memorialized the facts, and each conspirator received a full marquisate; the court forwarded gold and silk to reward Du Mao, Guo Liang's officers, and the people of Pingcheng who had come over.

sentences.zh: 自是盧芳城邑稍稍來降，涼誅其豪右郇氏之屬，鎮撫贏弱，旬月間雁門且平，芳遂亡入匈奴。

translations.literal: From this Lu Fang's cities gradually came to surrender; Liang executed powerful magnates such as the Xun clan, pacified the weak and worn; within a month Yanmen was nearly pacified, and Fang thereupon fled into the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang's towns then began to yield. Guo Liang executed leading families like the Xuns, protected the helpless, and within a month had nearly pacified Yanmen; Lu Fang fled to the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 帝擢涼子為中郎，宿衛左右。

translations.literal: The Emperor promoted Liang's son to middle general to guard left and right on night duty.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu promoted Guo Liang's son to middle general of the palace guard.

sentences.zh: 涼字公文，右北平人也。

translations.literal: Liang, courtesy name Gongwen, was a native of Youbeiping.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Liang, styled Gongwen, came from Youbeiping commandery.

sentences.zh: 身長八尺，氣力壯猛，雖武將，然通經書，多智略，尤曉邊事，有名北方。

translations.literal: His body was eight chi in height, his breath and strength bold and fierce; although a military general, yet he penetrated the classics and histories, had much strategy, especially understood border affairs, and had a name in the north.
translations.idiomatic: He stood eight chi tall, with the frame of a champion. Though a fighting man, he knew the classics, thought tactically, and was famed along the northern frontier for knowing border affairs.

sentences.zh: 初，幽州牧朱浮辟為兵曹掾，擊彭寵有功，封廣武侯。

translations.literal: At first Governor of Youzhou Zhu Fu summoned him as registrar of military affairs; he struck Peng Chong with merit and was enfeoffed as marquis of Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Zhu Fu of Youzhou first hired him as military registrar; he earned his spurs against Peng Chong and became marquis of Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 十三年，增茂邑，更封脩侯。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year Mao's fief was increased and he was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Xiu.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 13 Du Mao's stipend was raised and his title shifted to the marquisate of Xiu.

sentences.zh: 十五年，坐斷兵馬稟縑，使軍吏殺人，免官，削戶邑，定封參蘧鄉侯。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year he sat because he cut off army horse fodder and silk rations and caused a military clerk to kill someone; he was dismissed from office, households of the fief were cut, and the fief was fixed as marquis of Shenquxiang.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 15 he was cashiered for diverting army horses and silk rations and for a clerk's killing in the affair; his fief was trimmed to the marquisate of Shenquxiang.

sentences.zh: 十九年，卒。

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Jianwu 19.

sentences.zh: 子元嗣，永平十四年，坐與東平王等謀反，減死一等，國除。

translations.literal: His son Yuan succeeded; in the fourteenth year of Yongping, because he sat in conspiracy with the Prince of Dongping and others to rebel, death was reduced one grade, and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: His son Du Yuan inherited but lost the title in Yongping 14 for plotting with the Prince of Dongping; his sentence was commuted one degree from death.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后紹封茂孫奉為安樂亭侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Dowager Deng continued the line and enfeoffed Mao's grandson Feng as marquis of Anleting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Dowager Deng restored the line, naming Du Mao's grandson Du Feng marquis of Anleting.

sentences.zh: 馬成字君遷，南陽棘陽人也。

translations.literal: Ma Cheng, courtesy name Junqian, was a native of Jiyang in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Cheng, styled Junqian, came from Jiyang in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 少為縣吏。

translations.literal: In youth he was a county clerk.
translations.idiomatic: He began as a county clerk.

sentences.zh: 世祖徇潁川，以成為安集掾，調守郟令。

translations.literal: When the Shizu toured Yingchuan, he made Cheng adjutant for Anji and transferred him to act as magistrate of Jia.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu moved through Yingchuan he made Ma Cheng an adjutant for pacification, then acting magistrate of Jia.

sentences.zh: 及世祖討河北，成即棄官步負，追及於蒲陽，以成為期門，從征伐。

translations.literal: When the Shizu campaigned in Hebei, Cheng at once abandoned office, walked bearing supplies on his back, caught up at Puyang, was made a gentleman-at-palace-gate, and followed in campaigns.
translations.idiomatic: At the opening of the Hebei campaign Ma Cheng resigned, shouldered his kit, overtook the army at Puyang, entered the gentlemen-at-the-gates corps, and fought through the wars.

sentences.zh: 世祖即位，再遷護軍都尉。

translations.literal: When the Shizu ascended the throne, he was twice promoted to commandant of the protector guard.
translations.idiomatic: After the accession he rose twice to commandant of the protector guard.

sentences.zh: 建武四年，拜揚武將軍，督誅虜將軍劉隆、振威將軍宋登、射聲校尉王賞，發會稽、丹陽、九江、六安四郡兵擊李憲，時帝幸壽春，設壇場，祖禮遣之。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Jianwu he was appointed General Who Spreads Martial Might, supervising General Who Slays Captives Liu Long, General Who Shakes Might Song Deng, and Colonel of the Sound Archers Wang Shang, raising troops from the four commanderies Kuaiji, Danyang, Jiujiang, and Lu'an to strike Li Xian; at the time the Emperor visited Shouchun, set up an altar field, performed the farewell rite, and dispatched them.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 4 he became General Who Spreads Martial Might over Liu Long, Song Deng, and Wang Shang, mobilizing Kuaiji, Danyang, Jiujiang, and Lu'an against Li Xian. Emperor Guangwu came to Shouchun, built an altar, offered the parting sacrifice, and sent them off.

sentences.zh: 進圍憲於舒，令諸軍各深溝高壘。

translations.literal: He advanced to besiege Li Xian at Shu and ordered the various armies each to dig deep moats and raise high walls.
translations.idiomatic: He ringed Li Xian at Shu and ordered every column to dig in behind deep ditches and high ramparts.

sentences.zh: 憲數挑戰，成堅壁不出，守之歲餘。

translations.literal: Li Xian repeatedly challenged battle; Cheng held the walls fast and did not emerge, guarding for over a year.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian taunted them daily; Ma Cheng refused to give battle and held the lines for more than a year.

sentences.zh: 至六年春，城中食盡，乃攻之，遂屠舒，斬李憲，追及其黨與，盡平江淮地。

translations.literal: By the spring of the sixth year food in the city was exhausted; then they attacked, thereupon sacked Shu, beheaded Li Xian, pursued his faction, and entirely pacified the Jiang-Huai region.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 6 starvation forced the issue; they stormed Shu, executed Li Xian, ran his followers to earth, and cleared the Huai and Yangzi basins.

sentences.zh: 七年夏，封平舒侯。

translations.literal: In the summer of the seventh year he was enfeoffed as marquis of Pingshu.
translations.idiomatic: That summer he was enfeoffed as marquis of Pingshu.

sentences.zh: 八年，從征破隗囂，以成為天水太守，將軍如故。

translations.literal: In the eighth year he followed in the campaign that broke Wei Ao; Cheng was made governor of Tianshui, general as before.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 8 he took part in the defeat of Wei Ao and was named governor of Tianshui while keeping his general's commission.

sentences.zh: 冬，征還京師。

translations.literal: In winter he was summoned back to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: He was recalled to the capital that winter.

sentences.zh: 九年，代來歙守中郎將，率武威將軍劉尚等破河池，遂平武都。

translations.literal: In the ninth year he replaced Lai Xi as middle general, led General Who Commands Wuwei Liu Shang and others in breaking Hecheng, and thereupon pacified Wudu.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 9 he succeeded Lai Xi as middle general, joined Liu Shang in taking Hecheng, and completed the pacification of Wudu.

sentences.zh: 明年，大司空李通罷，以成行大司空事，居府如真，數月復拜揚武將軍。

translations.literal: The next year Grand Minister of Works Li Tong was dismissed, and Cheng was ordered to handle the affairs of grand minister of works, dwelling in the office as if substantive; after several months he was again appointed General Who Spreads Martial Might.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, when Li Tong stepped down as grand minister of works, Ma Cheng ran that ministry in an acting capacity for months before returning to his old title of General Who Spreads Martial Might.

sentences.zh: 十四年，屯常山、中山以備北邊，並領建義大將軍朱祐營。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year he encamped at Changshan and Zhongshan to prepare the northern border, and concurrently led the camp of Grand General Who Establishes Righteousness Zhu You.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 14 he garrisoned Changshan and Zhongshan against the north and also took charge of Zhu You's encampment.

sentences.zh: 又代驃騎大將軍杜茂繕治障塞，自西河至渭橋，河上至安邑，太原至井陘，中山至鄴，皆築保壁，起烽燧，十里一候。

translations.literal: He also replaced Grand General of Agile Cavalry Du Mao in repairing barriers and passes, from Xihe to Weiqiao, from He'shang to Anyi, from Taiyuan to Jingxing, from Zhongshan to Ye, all building fortified walls and raising beacon fires, one watch post every ten li.
translations.idiomatic: He succeeded Du Mao in refurbishing the frontier works from Xihe to the Wei bridge, from the He'nan shore to Anyi, from Taiyuan to Jingxing, and from Zhongshan to Ye—watchtowers and signal fires every ten li.

sentences.zh: 在事五六年，帝以成勤勞，征還京師。

translations.literal: In office five or six years, the Emperor because Cheng was diligent and toilsome summoned him back to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: After five or six years of this labor Guangwu recalled him to reward his exertions.

sentences.zh: 邊人多上書求請者，復遣成還屯。

translations.literal: Border people many submitted memorials begging; he again sent Cheng back to encamp.
translations.idiomatic: So many frontier petitions begged for his return that Guangwu sent him back to the line.

sentences.zh: 及南單于保塞，北方無事，拜為中山太守，上將軍印綬，領屯兵如故。

translations.literal: When the Southern Shanyu guarded the frontier passes and the north was without incident, he was appointed governor of Zhongshan, submitted the great general's seal and ribbon, and as before led encamped troops.
translations.idiomatic: Once the Southern Shanyu guarded the passes and the north grew quiet, he became governor of Zhongshan, gave up his general's seal, but kept command of the garrison.

sentences.zh: 二十四年，南擊武谿蠻賊，無功，上太守印綬。

translations.literal: In the twenty-fourth year he struck south against the bandits of Wuxi Man without merit and submitted the governor's seal and ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 24 he campaigned without success against the Wuxi tribes in the south and resigned his governorship.

sentences.zh: 二十七年，定封全椒侯。

translations.literal: In the twenty-seventh year his fief was fixed as marquis of Quanjiao.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 27 his title was fixed as marquis of Quanjiao.

sentences.zh: 就國。

translations.literal: He went to his state.
translations.idiomatic: He retired to his fief.

sentences.zh: 三十二年卒。

translations.literal: In the thirty-second year he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Jianwu 32.

sentences.zh: 子衛嗣。

translations.literal: His son Wei succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Ma Wei inherited.

sentences.zh: 衛卒，子香嗣，徙封棘陵侯。

translations.literal: When Wei died, his son Xiang succeeded, and the fief was moved to marquis of Jiling.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Wei was succeeded by Ma Xiang, who was transferred to the marquisate of Jiling.

sentences.zh: 香卒，子豐嗣。

translations.literal: When Xiang died, his son Feng succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Xiang died, his son Ma Feng inherited.

sentences.zh: 豐卒，子玄嗣。

translations.literal: When Feng died, his son Xuan succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Feng died, his son Ma Xuan inherited.

sentences.zh: 玄卒，子邑嗣。

translations.literal: When Xuan died, his son Yi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Xuan died, his son Ma Yi inherited.

sentences.zh: 邑卒，子醜嗣，桓帝時以罪失國。

translations.literal: When Yi died, his son Chou succeeded; in the time of Emperor Huan he lost the state because of a crime.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yi was followed by Ma Chou, who lost the fief under Emperor Huan for a criminal offense.

sentences.zh: 延熹二年，帝復封成玄孫昌為益陽亭侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yanxi the Emperor again enfeoffed Cheng's great-great-grandson Chang as marquis of Yiyangting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanxi 2 Emperor Huan revived the line with Ma Cheng's descendant Ma Chang as marquis of Yiyangting.

sentences.zh: 劉隆字元伯，南陽安眾侯宗室也。

translations.literal: Liu Long, courtesy name Yuanbo, was of the imperial lineage of the Marquis of Anzhong in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Long, styled Yuanbo, belonged to the imperial cadet branch of the marquises of Anzhong in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 王莽居攝中，隆父禮與安眾侯崇起兵誅莽，事泄，隆以年未七歲，故得免。

translations.literal: In Wang Mang's regency, Long's father Li together with the Marquis of Anzhong Chong raised troops to execute Mang; the affair leaked; Long because his age had not reached seven years therefore obtained exemption.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang's regency his father Liu Li had joined Marquis Chong of Anzhong in a plot to kill the usurper; when it failed the boy Liu Long was spared because he was not yet seven.

sentences.zh: 及壯，學於長安，更始拜為騎都尉。

translations.literal: When grown, he studied in Chang'an; Gengshi appointed him colonel of cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: As an adult he studied at Chang'an and received a colonel's commission from the Gengshi court.

sentences.zh: 謁歸，迎妻、子置洛陽。

translations.literal: On leave returning, he welcomed wife and children and settled them in Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: On home leave he brought his wife and children to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 聞世祖在河內，即追及於射犬，以為騎都尉，與馮異共拒朱鮪、李軼等，軼遂殺隆妻、子。

translations.literal: Hearing the Shizu was in Henei, he at once caught up at Shequan and was made colonel of cavalry; together with Feng Yi he resisted Zhu You and Li Yi and others, and Yi thereupon killed Long's wife and children.
translations.idiomatic: Learning that Liu Xiu was in Henei, he overtook him at Shequan, became a cavalry colonel, and with Feng Yi held the line against Zhu You and Li Yi—who murdered Liu Long's wife and children.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，封亢父侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu he was enfeoffed as marquis of Kangfu.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 he was enfeoffed as marquis of Kangfu.

sentences.zh: 四年，拜誅虜將軍，討李憲。

translations.literal: In the fourth year he was appointed General Who Slays Captives and campaigned against Li Xian.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 4 he became General Who Slays Captives against Li Xian.

sentences.zh: 憲平，遣隆屯田武當。

translations.literal: When Xian was pacified, Long was sent to garrison-field at Wudang.
translations.idiomatic: After Li Xian fell he was sent to open military colonies at Wudang.

sentences.zh: 十一年，守南郡太守，歲餘，上將軍印綬。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year he acted as governor of Nan commandery; after more than a year he submitted the great general's seal and ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 11 he served as acting governor of Nan for a year, then surrendered his general's seal.

sentences.zh: 十三年，增邑，更封竟陵侯。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year his fief was increased and he was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Jingling.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 13 his stipend rose and his title became marquis of Jingling.

sentences.zh: 是時，天下墾田多不以實，又戶口年紀互有增減。

translations.literal: At that time registered fields in the realm mostly did not accord with reality, and moreover household registers and ages mutually had increases and decreases.
translations.idiomatic: Across the empire land registers lied about acreage and household rolls inflated or hid ages.

sentences.zh: 十五年，詔下州郡檢核其事，而刺史、太守多不平均，或優饒豪右，侵刻羸弱，百姓嗟怨，遮道號呼。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year an edict went down to commanderies and provinces to inspect and verify these matters, but governors and shepherds mostly were not even-handed, some favoring powerful magnates and carving the weak and worn; the hundred surnames groaned in resentment and blocked the roads crying out.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 15 an edict ordered a full audit, but many governors played favorites, coddled magnates, and squeezed the poor until people mobbed the highways in protest.

sentences.zh: 時，諸郡各遣使奏事，帝見陳留吏牘上有書，視之，云：『潁川、弘農可問，河南、南陽不可問。

translations.literal: At that time each commandery sent envoys to memorialize affairs; the Emperor saw on the Chenliu clerk's document there was writing; looking at it, it said: "Yingchuan and Hongnong may be questioned; Henan and Nanyang may not be questioned."
translations.idiomatic: When each circuit sent clerks to report, Guangwu noticed a scrap on a Chenliu file: "Audit Yingchuan and Hongnong—but not Henan or Nanyang."

sentences.zh: 』帝詰吏由趣，吏不肯服，抵言於長壽街上得之。

translations.literal: The Emperor questioned the clerk as to the reason; the clerk would not submit and falsely said he had obtained it on Changshou street.
translations.idiomatic: He demanded an explanation; the clerk stonewalled and claimed he had picked it up on Changshou Street.

sentences.zh: 帝怒。

translations.literal: The Emperor was angry.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor flared with anger.

sentences.zh: 時，顯宗為東海公，年十二，在幄後言曰：『吏受郡敕，當欲以墾田相方耳。

translations.literal: At that time the Xianzong was Duke of Donghai, age twelve, and from behind the curtain said: "The clerk received the commandery's order; he must have meant to compare registered fields to each other."
translations.idiomatic: Twelve-year-old Liu Zhuang, the crown prince as Duke of Donghai, spoke from behind the curtain: "The clerk was only repeating what his superiors told him—about comparing land returns."

sentences.zh: 』帝曰：『即如此，何故言河南、南陽不可問？

translations.literal: The Emperor said: "If it is so, why does it say Henan and Nanyang may not be questioned?"
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu pressed, "If that is what he meant, why did the note say Henan and Nanyang must not be questioned?"

sentences.zh: 』對曰：『河南帝城，多近臣，南陽帝鄉，多近親，田宅逾制，不可為準。

translations.literal: He replied: "Henan is the imperial city, with many close ministers; Nanyang is the imperial home region, with many close kin; fields and houses exceed regulation and cannot be taken as a standard."
translations.idiomatic: The boy answered, "Henan is the capital swarm of palace favorites; Nanyang is the imperial clan's home—both places where estates overshoot the law. No honest tally is possible there."

sentences.zh: 』帝令虎賁將詰問吏，吏乃實首服，如顯宗對。

translations.literal: The Emperor ordered the general of the household rapid as tiger to question the clerk; the clerk then truly confessed submission, as the Xianzong had replied.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu had a rapid-as-tiger general grill the clerk until he confessed exactly as the crown prince had said.

sentences.zh: 於是遣謁者考實，具知奸狀。

translations.literal: Thereupon he sent yezhe to examine the truth and fully knew the treacherous conditions.
translations.idiomatic: Yezhe were sent to verify the fraud in every circuit.

sentences.zh: 明年，隆坐征下獄，其疇輩十餘人皆死。

translations.literal: The next year Long sat in investigation and was cast into prison; his colleagues over ten persons all died.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Liu Long went to prison over the audit; more than ten of his associates were executed.

sentences.zh: 帝以隆功臣，物免為庶人。

translations.literal: The Emperor because Long was a meritorious minister specially exempted him to commoner status.
translations.idiomatic: As a founding merit holder Liu Long was spared death and reduced to commoner rank.

sentences.zh: 明年，復封為扶樂鄉侯，以中郎將副伏波將軍馬援擊交阯蠻夷徵側等，隆別於禁谿口破之，獲其帥徵貳，斬首千餘級，降者二萬餘人。

translations.literal: The next year he was again enfeoffed as marquis of Fulexiang, as middle general assisting General Who Crosses the Waves Ma Yuan in striking the Man and Yi of Jiaozhi, Zheng Ce and others; Long separately at the mouth of Jinxikou broke them, captured their chieftain Zheng Er, beheaded over a thousand grades, and those who submitted were over twenty thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The following year he was restored as marquis of Fulexiang and sent as middle general under Ma Yuan against the Trung sisters in Jiaozhi; he routed a wing at Jinxikou, captured Zheng Er, took more than a thousand heads, and accepted the surrender of over twenty thousand.

sentences.zh: 還，更封大國，為長平侯。

translations.literal: On return he was enfeoffed in a great state as marquis of Changping.
translations.idiomatic: On his return his fief was enlarged to the great marquisate of Changping.

sentences.zh: 及大司馬吳漢薨，隆為驃騎將軍，行大司馬事。

translations.literal: When Grand Marshal Wu Han died, Long became general of agile cavalry and handled the affairs of grand marshal.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Han died Liu Long became general of agile cavalry and acting grand marshal.

sentences.zh: 隆奉法自守，視事八歲，上將軍印綬，罷，賜養牛，上樽酒十斛，以列侯奉朝請。

translations.literal: Long upheld the law and guarded himself; in eight years of handling affairs he submitted the great general's seal and ribbon, was dismissed, was gifted a draft ox and ten hu of fine wine, and as full marquis attended court audience.
translations.idiomatic: For eight years he ran the ministry by the book, then resigned the generalship, received an ox and ten hu of vintage wine, and stayed on as a supernumerary marquis.

sentences.zh: 三十年，定封慎侯。

translations.literal: In the thirtieth year his fief was fixed as marquis of Shen.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 30 his title was fixed as marquis of Shen.

sentences.zh: 中元二年，卒，謚曰靖侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Zhongyuan he died; his posthumous title was Marquis Jing.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Zhongyuan 2 with the posthumous epithet Jing, "The Steady."

sentences.zh: 子安嗣。

translations.literal: His son An succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Liu An inherited.

sentences.zh: 傅俊字子衛，潁川襄城人也。

translations.literal: Fu Jun, courtesy name Ziwei, was a native of Xiangcheng in Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Jun, styled Ziwei, came from Xiangcheng in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 世祖徇襄城，俊以縣亭長迎軍，拜為校尉，襄城收其母弟宗族，皆滅之。

translations.literal: When the Shizu toured Xiangcheng, Jun as district pavilion chief welcomed the army and was appointed colonel; Xiangcheng seized his mother, younger brothers, and lineage group, and all were exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu passed through Xiangcheng, Fu Jun, then a pavilion chief, brought the army in and was made a colonel—whereupon Wang Mang's men seized his mother, brothers, and entire clan and put them to death.

sentences.zh: 從破王尋等，以為偏將軍。

translations.literal: He followed in breaking Wang Xun and others and was made lieutenant general.
translations.idiomatic: He fought in the victory over Wang Xun and became lieutenant general.

sentences.zh: 別擊京、密，破之，遣歸潁川，收葬家屬。

translations.literal: Separately he struck Jing and Mi, broke them, was sent back to Yingchuan, and gathered and buried family members.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned separately against Jing and Mi, then returned to Yingchuan to bury his slaughtered kin.

sentences.zh: 子昌嗣，徙封蕪湖侯。

translations.literal: His son Chang succeeded and was moved in fief to marquis of Wuhu.
translations.idiomatic: His son Fu Chang inherited and was transferred to the marquisate of Wuhu.

sentences.zh: 建初中，遭母憂，因上書，以國貧不願之封，乞錢五十萬，為關內侯。

translations.literal: In the middle of Jianchu, encountering mourning for his mother, he therefore memorialized that because the state was poor he did not wish its fief and begged five hundred thousand cash to become marquis within the Pass.
translations.idiomatic: During mourning for his mother in the Jianchu era Fu Chang asked to give up his poor fief for five hundred thousand cash and a marquisate within the Pass instead.

sentences.zh: 肅宗怒，貶為關內侯，竟不賜錢。

translations.literal: The Suzong was angry, degraded him to marquis within the Pass, and in the end did not grant the cash.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang was furious, cut him to a mere marquis within the Pass, and refused the money.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后復封昌子鐵為高置亭侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Dowager Deng again enfeoffed Chang's son Tie as marquis of Gaozhiting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Dowager Deng restored Fu Chang's son Fu Tie as marquis of Gaozhiting.

sentences.zh: 堅鐔字子伋，潁川襄城人也。

translations.literal: Jian Tan, courtesy name Ziji, was a native of Xiangcheng in Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Jian Tan, styled Ziji, came from Xiangcheng in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 為郡縣吏。

translations.literal: He was a commandery and county clerk.
translations.idiomatic: He had served as a local clerk.

sentences.zh: 世祖討河北，或薦鐔者，因得召見。

translations.literal: When the Shizu campaigned in Hebei, someone recommended Tan, and for that reason he obtained summons and audience.
translations.idiomatic: During the Hebei campaign someone recommended him to Liu Xiu, who summoned him for an audience.

sentences.zh: 以其吏能，署主簿。

translations.literal: Because of his clerk ability, he was assigned chief clerk.
translations.idiomatic: His administrative skill won him appointment as chief clerk.

sentences.zh: 又拜偏將軍，從平河北，別擊破大槍於盧奴。

translations.literal: He was again appointed lieutenant general, followed in pacifying Hebei, and separately struck and broke the Great Lance at Lunu.
translations.idiomatic: He was commissioned lieutenant general, helped pacify Hebei, and on his own defeated the Great Lance band at Lunu.

sentences.zh: 世祖即位，拜鐔揚化將軍，封㶏強侯。

translations.literal: When the Shizu ascended the throne, he appointed Tan General Who Spreads Transformation and enfeoffed him as marquis of Yingqiang.
translations.idiomatic: At the accession he became General Who Spreads Transformation and marquis of Yingqiang.

sentences.zh: 與諸將攻洛陽，而朱鮪別將守東城者為反間，私約鐔晨開上東門。

translations.literal: Together with the generals he attacked Luoyang, and Zhu You's detached general who guarded the eastern city carried out a ruse, privately agreeing with Tan to open the upper east gate at dawn.
translations.idiomatic: During the siege of Luoyang Zhu You's officer on the east wall secretly agreed to open the upper east gate for Jian Tan at dawn.

sentences.zh: 鐔與建義大將軍朱祐乘朝而入，與鮪大戰武庫下，殺傷甚眾，至旦食乃罷，朱鮪由是遂降。

translations.literal: Tan together with Grand General Who Establishes Righteousness Zhu You rode in at dawn and fought a great battle with Zhu You below the armory, killing and wounding very many; only at breakfast time did they stop, and the latter Zhu You thereupon submitted.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn Jian Tan rode in with Grand General Zhu You, who held for Emperor Guangwu, and together they fought the Luoyang commander Zhu You, who still served Wang Mang's regime, below the armory until mid-morning, when the Luoyang commander capitulated.

sentences.zh: 又別擊內黃，平之。

translations.literal: He again separately struck Neihuang and pacified it.
translations.idiomatic: He next took Neihuang on an independent sortie.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，與右將軍萬脩徇南陽諸縣，而堵鄉人董訢反宛城，獲南陽太守劉驎。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu together with Right General Wan Xiu he toured the counties of Nanyang, while the man of Duxiang Dong Xin rebelled at Wancheng and captured Governor of Nanyang Liu Lin.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 he and Wan Xiu cleared the Nanyang counties while Dong Xin of Duxiang seized Wancheng and captured Governor Liu Lin.

sentences.zh: 鐔乃引軍赴宛，選敢死士夜自登城，斬關而入，訢遂棄城走還堵鄉。

translations.literal: Tan thereupon led the army to Wancheng, selected dare-to-die soldiers who by night climbed the city themselves, cut the bar and entered, and Xin thereupon abandoned the city and fled back to Duxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Jian Tan rushed to Wancheng, sent volunteers scaling the walls by night to cut the inner gate, and drove Dong Xin back to Duxiang.

sentences.zh: 鄧奉復反新野，攻破吳漢。

translations.literal: Deng Feng again rebelled at Xinye and broke Wu Han.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Feng then rose again at Xinye and shattered Wu Han.

sentences.zh: 時，萬脩病卒，鐔獨孤絕，南拒鄧奉，北當董訢，一年間道路隔塞，糧饋不至，鐔食蔬菜，與士卒共勞苦。

translations.literal: At that time Wan Xiu died of illness; Tan alone was isolated, south resisting Deng Feng, north blocking Dong Xin; within a year roads were cut off and supplies did not arrive; Tan ate vegetables and shared toil and hardship with soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: When Wan Xiu died of illness, Jian Tan stood alone between Deng Feng to the south and Dong Xin to the north; for a year the roads were closed and no convoys came—he lived on greens and shared every hardship with his men.

sentences.zh: 每急，輒先當矢石，身被三創，以此能全其眾。

translations.literal: Whenever it was urgent, he always first faced arrows and stones, his body receiving three wounds; by this he was able to preserve his host whole.
translations.idiomatic: In every crisis he took the arrow fire first, was wounded three times, and kept his command intact.

sentences.zh: 及帝征南陽，擊破訢、奉，以鐔為左曹，常從征伐。

translations.literal: When the Emperor campaigned in Nanyang and broke Xin and Feng, he made Tan left aide-de-camp and constantly followed in campaigns.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu marched south and broke Dong Xin and Deng Feng, Jian Tan became left aide-de-camp and fought at his side thereafter.

sentences.zh: 六年，定封合肥候。

translations.literal: In the sixth year his fief was fixed as marquis of Hefei.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 6 his title was fixed as marquis of Hefei.

sentences.zh: 二十六年，卒。

translations.literal: In the twenty-sixth year he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Jianwu 26.

sentences.zh: 子鴻嗣。

translations.literal: His son Hong succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Jian Hong inherited.

sentences.zh: 鴻卒，子浮嗣。

translations.literal: When Hong died, his son Fu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Jian Hong died, his son Jian Fu inherited.

sentences.zh: 浮卒，子雅嗣。

translations.literal: When Fu died, his son Ya succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Jian Fu died, his son Jian Ya inherited.

sentences.zh: 馬武字子張，南陽湖陽人也。

translations.literal: Ma Wu, courtesy name Zizhang, was a native of Huyang in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Wu, styled Zizhang, came from Huyang in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 少時避仇，客居江夏。

translations.literal: In youth he avoided a blood feud and lived as a guest in Jiangxia.
translations.idiomatic: As a young man he fled a vendetta and took refuge in Jiangxia.

sentences.zh: 王莽末，竟陵、西陽三老起兵於郡界，武往從之，後入綠林中，遂與漢軍合。

translations.literal: At the end of Wang Mang, the elders of Jingling and Xiyang raised troops within the commandery borders; Wu went to follow them, later entered the Green Woods, and thereupon joined with Han troops.
translations.idiomatic: Near the end of Wang Mang's reign the elders of Jingling and Xiyang rose in the commandery; Ma Wu joined them, entered the Green Woods band, and later merged with the Han armies.

sentences.zh: 更始立，以武為侍郎，與世祖破王尋等。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, he made Wu gentleman attendant and together with the Shizu broke Wang Xun and others.
translations.idiomatic: The Gengshi Emperor made him a gentleman attendant; he fought beside Liu Xiu to defeat Wang Xun.

sentences.zh: 拜為振威將軍，與尚書令謝躬共攻王郎。

translations.literal: He was appointed General Who Shakes Might and together with Director of the Secretariat Xie Gong attacked Wang Lang.
translations.idiomatic: He was named General Who Shakes Might and joined Xie Gong, director of the Secretariat, against Wang Lang.

sentences.zh: 及世祖拔邯鄲，請躬及武等置酒高會，因欲以圖躬，不克。

translations.literal: When the Shizu took Handan, he invited Gong and Wu and others to set wine in a lofty gathering and for that reason wished to plot against Gong; it did not succeed.
translations.idiomatic: After Liu Xiu took Handan he feasted Xie Gong, Ma Wu, and the rest, hoping to trap Xie Gong in the same stroke; the plot failed.

sentences.zh: 既罷，獨與武登叢臺，從容謂武曰：『吾得漁陽、上谷突騎，欲令將軍將之，何如？

translations.literal: When it was finished, he alone with Wu ascended the Cong Terrace and said at ease: "I have obtained the shock cavalry of Yuyang and Shanggu and wish to order the general to command them—what of it?"
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Liu Xiu led Ma Wu alone up the Cong Terrace and said, "I have Yuyang and Shanggu shock cavalry in hand and want you to lead them—will you?"

sentences.zh: 』武曰：『駑怯無方略。

translations.literal: Wu said: "I am dull and timid and lack strategy."
translations.idiomatic: Ma Wu answered, "I am too dull and timid for that kind of command."

sentences.zh: 』世祖曰：『將軍久將，習兵，豈與我掾史同哉！

translations.literal: The Shizu said: "The general has long commanded and is practiced in arms—how could he be the same as my clerks and aides!"
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiu replied, "You have led troops for years—you are no mere clerk!"

sentences.zh: 』武由是歸心。

translations.literal: Wu from this devoted his heart.
translations.idiomatic: From that day Ma Wu gave him his full loyalty.

sentences.zh: 及謝躬誅死，武馳至射犬降，世祖見之甚悅，引置左右，每勞饗諸將，武輒起斟酌於前，世祖以為歡。

translations.literal: When Xie Gong was executed and died, Wu galloped to Shequan to submit; the Shizu saw him and was greatly pleased, led him and placed him left and right; whenever he feasted the generals in consolation, Wu always rose to pour wine in front, and the Shizu took this as joy.
translations.idiomatic: When Xie Gong was killed, Ma Wu raced to Shequan to surrender. Guangwu received him with delight, kept him at his side, and at every banquet for the generals Ma Wu would rise to pour the wine—a sight the emperor plainly enjoyed.

sentences.zh: 復使將其部曲至鄴，武叩頭辭以不願，世祖愈美其意，因從擊群賊。

translations.literal: Again he ordered him to lead his personal troops to Ye; Wu knocked his head declining that he was unwilling; the Shizu the more praised his intention and for that reason followed in striking various bandits.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu told him to take his old unit to Ye, Ma Wu kowtowed and begged off. The emperor admired his scruple all the more and took him along against the remaining bandits instead.

sentences.zh: 世祖擊尤來、五幡等，敗於慎水，武獨殿，還陷陣，故賊不得迫及。

translations.literal: When the Shizu struck Youlai and the Five Banners and others, he was defeated at Shenshui; Wu alone brought up the rear, returned and broke the lines, so the bandits could not pursue and catch up.
translations.idiomatic: In the rout at Shenshui against Youlai and the Five Banners, Ma Wu alone covered the retreat, wheeled back into the enemy, and kept the pursuers from closing.

sentences.zh: 進至安次、小廣陽，武常為軍鋒，力戰無前，諸將皆引而隨之，故遂破賊，窮追至平谷，浚靡而還。

translations.literal: Advancing to Anzi and Xiaoguangyang, Wu constantly acted as army spearhead, fighting hard without peer; the generals all drew back and followed him, and for that reason they thereupon broke the bandits, pursued to the limit to Pinggu, and returned after Junmi.
translations.idiomatic: At Anzi and Xiaoguangyang he spearheaded every charge until the other generals fell in behind him; they broke the enemy, ran them to Pinggu, and only then turned back from Junmi.

sentences.zh: 十三年，增邑，更封脩侯。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year his fief was increased and he was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Xiu.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 13 his stipend was raised and his title became marquis of Xiu.

sentences.zh: 將兵北屯下曲陽，備匈奴。

translations.literal: He led troops north to encamp at Xiaquyang to prepare against the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: He garrisoned Xiaquyang on the northern frontier against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 坐殺軍吏，受詔將妻子就國。

translations.literal: He sat because he killed a military clerk; he received an edict ordering him to lead wife and children to his state.
translations.idiomatic: He was convicted of killing an army clerk and ordered to retire to his fief with his family.

sentences.zh: 武徑詣洛陽，上將軍印綬，削戶五百，定封為楊虛侯，因留奉朝請。

translations.literal: Wu went straight to Luoyang, submitted the great general's seal and ribbon, had five hundred households cut from his fief, the fief was fixed as marquis of Yangxu, and for that reason remained as fengchaoqing.
translations.idiomatic: Instead he went straight to Luoyang, handed in his general's seal, took a cut of five hundred households, was fixed as marquis of Yangxu, and stayed on at court as a supernumerary.

sentences.zh: 帝後與功臣諸侯宴語，從容言曰：『諸卿不遭際會，自度爵祿何所至乎？

translations.literal: Later when the Emperor feasted and talked with meritorious ministers and marquises, he said at ease: "If you gentlemen had not encountered the conjunction, where would you yourselves have reckoned rank and salary would reach?"
translations.idiomatic: Later, at a banquet with the founding marquises, Guangwu asked idly, "If fate had never brought us together, what rank do you suppose each of you would have reached?"

sentences.zh: 』高密侯鄧禹先對曰：『臣少嘗學問，可郡文學博士。

translations.literal: Marquis of Gaomi Deng Yu first replied: "In youth I once studied learning; I could have been erudite or doctoral erudite of a commandery."
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu of Gaomi answered first, "I had some schooling—I might have ended a commandery erudite."

sentences.zh: 』帝曰：『何言之謙乎』卿鄧氏子，誌行修整，何為不掾功曹？

translations.literal: The Emperor said: "How modest is this speech! You are a son of the Deng clan, your will and conduct well ordered—why would you not be a clerk or merit officer?"
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu said, "Too modest by half! A Deng with your conduct would surely have risen to clerk or merit officer?"

sentences.zh: 』餘各以次對，至武，曰：『臣以武勇，可守尉督盜賊。

translations.literal: The rest each replied in order; when it came to Wu, he said: "I by martial courage could have guarded a commandery or been a captain who supervises bandits."
translations.idiomatic: The others answered in turn. Ma Wu said, "With my brawn I might have been a county captain chasing robbers."

sentences.zh: 』帝笑曰：『且勿為盜賊，自致亭長，斯可矣。

translations.literal: The Emperor laughed and said: "And do not be a bandit—rise to pavilion chief yourself, and that would be enough."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor laughed, "Just don't be the robber—maybe you'd have made pavilion chief—that would be plenty."

sentences.zh: 』武為人嗜酒，闊達敢言，時醉在御前面折同列，言其短長，無所避忌，帝故縱之，以為笑樂。

translations.literal: Wu as a man loved wine, was broad and outspoken; at times drunk he before the Emperor rebuked those of his rank, speaking their shortcomings and strengths, without avoiding taboo; the Emperor therefore indulged him and took it as laughter and pleasure.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Wu loved his wine and spoke his mind; drunk at court he would dress down his peers before the throne, sparing no one's faults. Guangwu indulged him for the entertainment.

sentences.zh: 帝雖制禦功臣，而每能回容，宥其小失。

translations.literal: Although the Emperor controlled meritorious ministers, yet each time could turn and contain them, pardoning their small faults.
translations.idiomatic: Though he kept his generals on a short leash, he usually glossed over their small slips.

sentences.zh: 遠方貢珍甘，必先遍賜列侯，而太官無餘。

translations.literal: When distant regions presented rare delicacies, he first everywhere gifted full marquises, and the imperial kitchen had no surplus.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute dainties from the provinces went first to every full marquis until the imperial kitchen had nothing left over.

sentences.zh: 有功，輒增邑賞，不任以吏職，故皆保其福祿，終無誅譴者。

translations.literal: When there was merit, he at once increased fiefs and rewards, and did not assign them clerk duties; therefore all preserved their blessings and salary, and in the end there were none executed or condemned.
translations.idiomatic: Merit brought fiefs and cash, not grinding civil posts, so most of them kept their fortunes and died unpunished.

sentences.zh: 二十五年，武以中郎將將兵擊武陵蠻夷，還，上印綬，顯宗初，西羌寇隴右，覆軍殺將，朝廷患之，復拜武捕虜將軍，以中郎將王豐副，與監軍使者竇固、右輔都尉陳訁斤，將烏桓、黎陽營、三輔募士、涼州諸郡羌胡兵及弛刑，合四萬人擊之。

translations.literal: In the twenty-fifth year Wu as middle general led troops to strike the Man and Yi of Wuling; on return he submitted the seal and ribbon. At the beginning of the Xianzong, the Western Qiang raided Longyou, overturned armies and killed generals, and the court worried over this; again he appointed Wu General Who Captures Captives, with Middle General Wang Feng as deputy, together with army overseer Dou Gu and right adjunct commandant Chen Xin, leading Wuhuan, the Liyang camp, recruited soldiers of the three adjuncts, Qiang and Hu troops of the various commanderies of Liangzhou and commuting sentences, in all forty thousand striking them.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 25 Ma Wu led an expedition as middle general against the tribes of Wuling and then resigned his commission. Early in Emperor Ming's reign the western Qiang overran Longyou, destroying armies and killing commanders. The court recalled Ma Wu as General Who Captures Captives with Wang Feng as his deputy, joined Dou Gu as army overseer and Chen Xin as right adjunct commandant, and sent forty thousand men—Wuhuan, Liyang garrison troops, recruits from the three adjuncts, Liangzhou tribal auxiliaries, and amnestied convicts—against them.

sentences.zh: 到金城浩，與羌戰，斬首六百級。

translations.literal: Reaching Haomen in Jincheng, he fought the Qiang and beheaded six hundred grades.
translations.idiomatic: At Haomen in Jincheng they killed six hundred Qiang.

sentences.zh: 又戰於洛都谷，為羌所敗，死者千餘人。

translations.literal: Again they fought at Luodu Valley and were defeated by the Qiang; over a thousand died.
translations.idiomatic: A second battle in Luodu Valley went badly; more than a thousand Han soldiers fell.

sentences.zh: 羌乃率眾引出塞，武復追擊到東、西邯，大破之，斬首四千六百級，獲生口千六百人，餘皆降散。

translations.literal: The Qiang thereupon led the mass and drew out beyond the passes; Wu again pursued and struck to east and west Han, greatly defeated them, beheaded four thousand six hundred grades, captured alive sixteen hundred persons, and the rest all scattered in submission.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang then withdrew beyond the frontier; Ma Wu pursued them to the Eastern and Western Han stockades, shattered them, took 4,600 heads, 1,600 prisoners, and scattered the rest into surrender.

sentences.zh: 武振旅還京師，增邑七百戶，並前千八百戶。

translations.literal: Wu shook the host and returned to the capital; his fief was increased seven hundred households, together with before one thousand eight hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: He marched home in good order and gained seven hundred new households, for a total of 1,800.

sentences.zh: 永平四年，卒。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yongping he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Yongping 4.

sentences.zh: 子檀嗣，坐兄伯濟與楚王英黨顏忠謀反，國除。

translations.literal: His son Tan succeeded; because elder brother Boji sat with Yan Zhong, partisan of the Prince of Chu Ying, in plotting rebellion, the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: His son Ma Tan inherited but lost the fief when his brother Ma Boji was implicated with Yan Zhong in Prince Ying of Chu's plot.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后紹封武孫震為漻亭侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Dowager Deng continued the line and enfeoffed Wu's grandson Zhen as marquis of Liaoting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Dowager Deng restored Ma Wu's grandson Ma Zhen as marquis of Liaoting.

sentences.zh: 震卒，子側嗣。

translations.literal: When Zhen died, his son Ce succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Zhen died, his son Ma Ce inherited.

sentences.zh: 論曰：中興二十八將，前世以為上應二十八宿，未之詳也。

translations.literal: The judgment says: The twenty-eight generals of the restoration—former ages thought them the upper match of the twenty-eight lunar lodges; this has not been examined in detail.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: tradition pairs the twenty-eight founding generals with the twenty-eight asterisms—a pretty notion that does not bear close scrutiny.

sentences.zh: 然咸能感會風雲，奮其智勇，稱為佐命，亦各誌能之士也。

translations.literal: Yet all were able to respond to the moment of wind and cloud, rouse their wisdom and courage, and were called helpers of the mandate—each also was a man of recorded ability.
translations.idiomatic: Yet each seized the storm of the age, brought wit and courage to bear, and earned the name of co-founder; they were men of real talent.

sentences.zh: 議者多非光武不以功臣任職，至使英姿茂績，委而勿用。

translations.literal: Those who debated mostly faulted Guangwu for not employing meritorious ministers in office, to the point that heroic bearing and lush achievement were cast aside and not used.
translations.idiomatic: Critics often blame Guangwu for refusing to put his generals in civil high office, wasting their brilliance on idle titles.

sentences.zh: 然原夫深圖遠算，固將有以焉爾。

translations.literal: Yet tracing the origin of deep plans and long reckoning, solidly there must have been reason therein.
translations.idiomatic: Yet his deep policy had its reasons.

sentences.zh: 若乃王道既衰，降及霸德，猶能授受惟庸，勛賢皆序，如管、隰之叠升桓世，先、趙之同列文朝，可謂兼通矣。

translations.literal: As for when the kingly way had already declined, descending to hegemonic virtue, still they could grant and receive according to merit, and meritorious worthies all had order, like Guan and Xi rising in succession in Huan's age, Xian and Zhao sharing rank in Wen's court—this can be called comprehending both.
translations.idiomatic: When true kingship faded into mere hegemony, houses like Qi still promoted Guan Zhong and Xi Peng in orderly succession, and Jin still ranked Xianchen and Zhao Shuai at Duke Wen's court—that was sharing power with merit wisely.

sentences.zh: 降自秦、漢，世資戰力，至於翼扶王運，皆武人屈起。

translations.literal: Descending from Qin and Han, generations relied on warlike force; as for winging and supporting the royal fortune, all were military men who rose abruptly.
translations.idiomatic: From Qin and Han onward, thrones were won by the sword; every dynasty that steadied the throne began with soldiers who sprang from nowhere.

sentences.zh: 亦有鬻繒屠狗輕猾之徒，或崇以連城之賞，或任以阿衡之地，故執疑則隙生，力侔則亂起。

translations.literal: There were also fellows who sold silk and butchered dogs, frivolous and slick; some were honored with rewards of linked cities, some were assigned the lands of the chief minister—therefore when suspicion was grasped, rifts arose; when strength matched, disorder rose.
translations.idiomatic: Peddlers and dog-butcher adventurers won whole provinces or the powers of a Yi Yin—then suspicion bred splits and matched strength bred civil war.

sentences.zh: 蕭、樊且猶縲紲，信、越終見菹戮，不其然乎！

translations.literal: Xiao and Fan were still bound in cords; Xin and Yue in the end saw pickle and execution—is it not so!
translations.idiomatic: Xiao He and Fan Kuai still faced prison; Han Xin and Peng Yue ended on the mincing block—and was that not inevitable!

sentences.zh: 自茲以降，迄於孝武，宰輔五世，莫非公侯。

translations.literal: From this descending, down to Xiaowu, over five generations of chief ministers, none were not dukes and marquises.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, down to Emperor Wu of Former Han, chief ministers for five generations running were all nobles.

sentences.zh: 遂使縉紳道塞，賢能蔽壅，朝有世及之私，下多抱關之怨。

translations.literal: Thereupon the way of the gentry was blocked, worthies were covered and obstructed; the court had the selfishness of hereditary succession, below there was much resentment of gatekeepers.
translations.idiomatic: The path of scholar-officials choked; talent could not rise while great offices stayed in noble houses and ordinary clerks nursed endless grievance.

sentences.zh: 其懷道無聞，委身草莽者，亦何可勝言。

translations.literal: Those who embraced the Way but were unheard of, who cast their persons into the grass and thickets—how could they be spoken to exhaustion.
translations.idiomatic: How many men of principle rotted unseen in the wilds.

sentences.zh: 故光武鑒前事之違，存矯枉之志，雖寇、鄧之高勛，耿、賈之鴻烈，分土不過大縣數四，所加特進、朝請而已。
