translations.literal: Therefore Guangwu took warning from the errors of former affairs and preserved the intent to straighten the bent; although Kou and Deng had lofty merit, Geng and Jia had vast achievement, divided land did not exceed several great counties of four, what was added was specially advanced and court audience only.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu studied those mistakes and set out to correct them: even Kou Xun and Deng Yu, Geng Yan and Jia Fu received fiefs of only a few large counties, with honorary titles such as specially advanced and court audience on top—nothing like an empire within the empire.

sentences.zh: 觀其治平臨政，課職責咎，將所謂『導之以政，齊之以刑』者乎！

translations.literal: Observe his governing peace and facing administration, assessing offices and assigning blame—is this not what is called "lead them with administration, align them with punishments"!
translations.idiomatic: Watch him rule a pacified realm, assign offices, and demand results—is that not the Confucian ideal of governing by policy and aligning conduct with law!

sentences.zh: 若格之功臣，其傷已甚。

translations.literal: If one applied the straight cord to meritorious ministers, the injury would already be very great.
translations.idiomatic: Yet to judge old comrades by the strict letter of the law would have broken every bond of gratitude.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: Why?
translations.idiomatic: Why was that?

sentences.zh: 直繩則虧喪恩舊，橈情則違廢禁典，選德則功不必厚，舉勞則人或未賢，參任則群心難塞，並列則其敝未遠。

translations.literal: The straight cord then injures and loses old kindness; bending feeling then violates and abandons prohibiting statutes; selecting virtue then merit need not be thick; raising toil then men may not yet be worthy; mixed appointment then the mass of hearts is hard to stop; side-by-side appointment then its harm is not yet distant.
translations.idiomatic: Strict justice wounds old friends; leniency breaks the code; picking only virtue ignores battlefield debt; rewarding only sweat elevates the unworthy; mixing civil and military breeds jealousy; duplicating offices courts disaster.

sentences.zh: 不得不校其勝否，即以事相權。

translations.literal: He could not but compare their wins and losses, and thereupon weigh them by affairs.
translations.idiomatic: A ruler had to weigh those trade-offs case by case.

sentences.zh: 故高秩厚禮，允答元功，峻文深憲，責成吏職。

translations.literal: Therefore high rank and thick gifts truly answered the first merit; stern texts and deep statutes assigned completion to clerk duties.
translations.idiomatic: So Guangwu paid his generals in rank and treasure, but left real administration to trained bureaucrats under strict law.

sentences.zh: 建武之世，侯者百餘，若夫數公者，則與參國議，分均休咎，其餘並優以寬科，完其封祿，莫不終以功名延慶於後。

translations.literal: In the Jianwu era marquises were over a hundred; as for several of those lords, they then joined in state counsel, sharing equally good and ill fortune; the rest were all favored with lenient statutes, completing their fiefs and salaries, and none failed in the end to extend celebration of merit and fame to posterity.
translations.idiomatic: More than a hundred held marquisates in the Jianwu years; a handful sat in council on great affairs of state, while the rest enjoyed light duties, kept their revenues, and almost all died honored and left prosperous heirs.

sentences.zh: 昔留侯以為高祖悉用蕭、曹故人，而郭伋亦譏南陽多顯，鄭興又戒功臣專任。

translations.literal: Formerly Marquis Liu thought Gaozu wholly used Xiao and Cao old acquaintances, and Guo Ji also ridiculed that Nanyang had many illustrious; Zheng Xing again warned against meritorious ministers monopolizing office.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Liang once warned that Gaozu packed his government with old friends from Pei; Guo Ji mocked the Nanyang clique; Zheng Xing cautioned against letting generals run the ministries.

sentences.zh: 夫崇恩偏授，易啟私溺之失，至公均被，必廣招賢之路，意者不其然乎！

translations.literal: When favor is exalted and partiality granted, it is easy to open the fault of private indulgence; when utmost fairness is equally spread, it must broadly summon the road of worthies—the intent is perhaps not thus!
translations.idiomatic: Favoritism rots a government, but even-handed openness to talent steadies it—Guangwu's policy was the wiser course!

sentences.zh: 永平中，顯宗追感前世功臣，乃圖畫二十八將於南宮雲臺，其外又有王常、李通、竇融、卓茂，合三十二人。

translations.literal: In the Yongping era the Xianzong recalled with gratitude the meritorious ministers of former generations, and therefore painted the twenty-eight generals on the Yuntai of the Southern Palace; outside them were also Wang Chang, Li Tong, Dou Rong, and Zhuo Mao—thirty-two persons in all.
translations.idiomatic: In the Yongping era Emperor Ming commemorated the founders by painting the twenty-eight generals on the Yuntai gallery of the Southern Palace, adding Wang Chang, Li Tong, Dou Rong, and Zhuo Mao for a total of thirty-two portraits.

sentences.zh: 故依其本弟系之篇末，以誌功臣之次云爾。

translations.literal: Therefore according to their original order he attached them at the end of the chapter, to record the order of meritorious ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Their names are therefore listed here in the order of that roll of honor.

sentences.zh: 太傅高密侯鄧禹、中山太守全椒侯馬成、大司馬廣平侯吳漢、河南尹阜成侯王梁、左將軍膠東侯賈復、瑯邪太守祝阿侯陳俊、建威大將軍好畤侯耿弇、驃騎大將軍參蘧侯杜茂、

translations.literal: Grand tutor Deng Yu, marquis of Gaomi; Zhongshan governor Ma Cheng, marquis of Quanjiao; grand marshal Wu Han, marquis of Guangping; Henan governor Wang Liang, marquis of Fucheng; left general Jia Fu, marquis of Jiaodong; Langye governor Chen Jun, marquis of Zhua; General Who Establishes Might Geng Yan, marquis of Hao; grand general of agile cavalry Du Mao, marquis of Shenqu;
translations.idiomatic: Grand tutor Deng Yu, marquis of Gaomi; Ma Cheng, governor of Zhongshan and marquis of Quanjiao; grand marshal Wu Han, marquis of Guangping; Wang Liang, governor of Henan and marquis of Fucheng; left general Jia Fu, marquis of Jiaodong; Chen Jun, governor of Langye and marquis of Zhua; Geng Yan, general who establishes might and marquis of Hao; Du Mao, grand general of agile cavalry and marquis of Shenqu;

sentences.zh: 執金吾雍奴侯寇恂、積弩將軍昆陽侯傅俊、征南大將軍舞陽侯岑彭、左曹合肥侯堅鐔、征西大將軍陽夏侯馮異、上谷太守淮陵侯王霸、建義大將軍鬲侯朱祐、信都太守阿陵侯任光、

translations.literal: Bearer of the gilded mace Kou Xun, marquis of Yongnu; general of accumulated crossbows Fu Jun, marquis of Kunyang; grand general who conquers the south Cen Peng, marquis of Wuyang; left aide-de-camp Jian Tan, marquis of Hefei; grand general who conquers the west Feng Yi, marquis of Yangxia; Shanggu governor Wang Ba, marquis of Huailing; Zhu You, grand general who establishes righteousness, marquis of Ge; Ren Guang, governor of Xindu and marquis of Aling;
translations.idiomatic: Kou Xun, bearer of the gilded mace and marquis of Yongnu; Fu Jun, general of accumulated crossbows and marquis of Kunyang; Cen Peng, grand general who conquers the south and marquis of Wuyang; Jian Tan, left aide-de-camp and marquis of Hefei; Feng Yi, grand general who conquers the west and marquis of Yangxia; Wang Ba, governor of Shanggu and marquis of Huailing; Zhu You, grand general who establishes righteousness and marquis of Ge; Ren Guang, governor of Xindu and marquis of Aling;

sentences.zh: 征虜將軍潁陽侯祭遵、豫章太守中水侯李忠、驃騎大將軍櫟陽侯景丹、右將軍槐里侯萬脩、虎牙大將軍安平侯蓋延、太常靈壽侯邳彤、衛尉安成侯銚期、驍騎將軍昌成侯劉植、

translations.literal: General who subdues captives Ji Zun, marquis of Yingyang; Li Zhong, governor of Yuzhang and marquis of Zhongshui; Jing Dan, grand general of agile cavalry and marquis of Liyang; Wan Xiu, right general and marquis of Huaili; Gai Yan, grand general of tiger fangs and marquis of Anping; Pi Tong, minister of ceremonies and marquis of Lingshou; Yao Qi, captain of the guard and marquis of Ancheng; Liu Zhi, general of nimble cavalry and marquis of Changcheng;
translations.idiomatic: Ji Zun, general who subdues captives and marquis of Yingyang; Li Zhong, governor of Yuzhang and marquis of Zhongshui; Jing Dan, grand general of agile cavalry and marquis of Liyang; Wan Xiu, right general and marquis of Huaili; Gai Yan, grand general of tiger fangs and marquis of Anping; Pi Tong, minister of ceremonies and marquis of Lingshou; Yao Qi, captain of the guard and marquis of Ancheng; Liu Zhi, general of nimble cavalry and marquis of Changcheng;

sentences.zh: 東郡太守東光侯耿純、橫野大將軍山桑侯王常、城門校尉朗陵侯臧宮、大司空固始侯李通、捕虜將軍楊虛侯馬武、大司空安豐侯竇融、驃騎將軍慎侯劉隆、太傅宣德侯卓茂。

translations.literal: Geng Chun, governor of Dongjun and marquis of Dongguang; Wang Chang, grand general who sweeps the wilderness and marquis of Shansang; Zang Gong, colonel of the gates and marquis of Langling; Li Tong, grand minister of works and marquis of Gushi; Ma Wu, general who captures captives and marquis of Yangxu; Dou Rong, grand minister of works and marquis of Anfeng; Liu Long, general of agile cavalry and marquis of Shen; Zhuo Mao, grand tutor and marquis of Xuande.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun, governor of Dongjun and marquis of Dongguang; Wang Chang, grand general who sweeps the wilderness and marquis of Shansang; Zang Gong, colonel of the gates and marquis of Langling; Li Tong, grand minister of works and marquis of Gushi; Ma Wu, general who captures captives and marquis of Yangxu; Dou Rong, grand minister of works and marquis of Anfeng; Liu Long, general of agile cavalry and marquis of Shen; Zhuo Mao, grand tutor and marquis of Xuande.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：帝績思乂，庸功是存。

translations.literal: The praise says: The Emperor's achievement thought of order; meritorious work is preserved.
translations.idiomatic: The summation sings: the emperor sought peace and kept faith with those who won it for him.

sentences.zh: 有來羣后，捷我戎軒。

translations.literal: There came the host of lords; they quickened our war chariots.
translations.idiomatic: The lords rallied to him and gave speed to his chariots of war.

sentences.zh: 婉孌龍姿，儷景同翻。

translations.literal: Lovely the dragon bearing; paired with the sunlight they turn together.
translations.idiomatic: Lovely as dragons in the royal likeness, they wheel with him in the same radiance.
